purpose: First fixed-case suite for fresh S0 benchmark plus near-online/post-run S2 diagnostic. This is not a full online S2 run.

cases.description: Teams bridge checkpoint: Dell PowerEdge R750 (Config 38)
cases.selection_reason: Low-cost pass control from the prior 5-case CSM/ITSM/Teams set; useful for false-block checks.
expected_observation_points: S0 remains a clean pass under the frozen runner settings.
S2 closure should allow with no missing actions.
S2 tool_state should not repair or block normalized Teams/CSM/ITSM calls.

cases.description: Post-release support preparation: invoice export null-guard
cases.selection_reason: Lowest-token pass control in the baseline report, covering Gitea/ITSM/Teams without Drive.
expected_observation_points: S0 pass establishes a low-cost control for Gitea/ITSM/Teams.
S2 tool_state should tolerate valid Gitea and ITSM calls without false repairs.
S2 closure should not mark post-release support evidence missing if S0 passes cleanly.

cases.description: CSM and internal guidance alignment: SQL Server Patchset 27 diagnostics
cases.selection_reason: Passing token-heavy control that exercises HR/ITSM/Teams-like delegation.
expected_observation_points: S0 may pass while still consuming very high tokens; treat this as an efficiency warning, not a ContextHub correctness win.
S2 should avoid false blocks on high-volume valid traces.
Report token and duration separately from correctness.

cases.description: HR and ITSM routing checkpoint: IT Security Best Practices
cases.selection_reason: Highest-token passing control; guards against overstating S2 on token-heavy but correct S0 behavior.
expected_observation_points: S0 pass should be preserved as a pass control.
S2 should not claim raw token reduction unless a separate budget guardrail is explicitly measured.
Any S2 repair/block here is a high-priority false-positive review.

cases.description: Teams bridge checkpoint: Palo Alto Networks PA-3220
cases.selection_reason: Prior timeout case already validated by the near-online S2 pilot as a closure block for missing KB update.
expected_observation_points: S0 timeout should be recorded as runtime/model behavior unless closure converts the partial trace into explicit unmet obligations.
S2 closure should identify missing workflow evidence such as knowledge_base_specialist.update_knowledge when applicable.
Tool_state is expected to mostly allow normalized calls; closure is the primary S2 signal.

cases.description: Teams bridge checkpoint: Premium Support SLA (24x7, Tier 25)
cases.selection_reason: Second timeout from the prior 5-case set; gives a non-token-anomaly timeout contrast to mcp_single_145.
expected_observation_points: S0 timeout and memory-clear behavior should be reported without attributing raw latency to ContextHub.
S2 closure should convert partial trace boundaries into missing action/evidence findings where supported.
Handoff remains partial and should not be described as full online interception.

cases.description: Premium support SLA risk: Premium Support SLA (24x7, Tier 25)
cases.selection_reason: High-token closure failure with repeated tool/planning loop; strong candidate for closure plus tool_state diagnostics.
expected_observation_points: S0 failure should be classified by missing closure evidence and loop behavior, not by timeout.
S2 closure should surface unmet KB/CSM obligations if they are absent from the trace.
S2 tool_state should be reviewed for repeated valid calls versus schema/object failures.

cases.description: Customer communications with internal bridge: Juniper EX4300 Variant 19
cases.selection_reason: Highest-token failed case; combines closure, wrong/missing tool args, and repeated loop signals.
expected_observation_points: S0 distribution should show this as a high-cost failure outlier.
S2 closure should identify missing evidence separately from tool argument/state violations.
Any S2 claim must be tied to concrete unmet obligations or validated tool_state findings.

cases.description: Case-to-knowledge reuse and closure prep: Juniper EX4300 Variant 19
cases.selection_reason: Low-cost closure/tool_state failure, useful for debugging without the cost profile of mcp_single_52 or mcp_single_61.
expected_observation_points: S0 failure should localize missing closure evidence versus wrong/missing tool args.
S2 closure and tool_state should produce interpretable findings at low runtime cost.
Useful early canary before running high-cost closure cases.

cases.description: Case-to-knowledge reuse and closure prep: NGINX Plus Variant 51
cases.selection_reason: Clear wrong/missing tool args case with only one failed agent; good focused tool_state candidate.
expected_observation_points: S0 failure should be tied to ITSM tool argument or object-state mismatch.
S2 tool_state should report schema/object findings with live-schema normalization.
Closure should not be overstated if the primary observable is tool args/object state.

cases.description: Drive runbook checkpoint: invoice export null-guard
cases.selection_reason: Drive/Gitea/ITSM failure with wrong/missing ITSM tool args; covers the Drive/Gitea path requested for the first suite.
expected_observation_points: S0 failure should identify the IT service desk tool/object mismatch.
S2 tool_state should validate Drive, Gitea, and ITSM calls against live schemas.
Report Drive/Gitea coverage separately from CSM/ITSM/Teams coverage.

cases.description: War-room email and calendar coordination: Hr Database Service Failure
cases.selection_reason: Wrong/missing HR tool args case that broadens tool_state coverage to HR plus calendar/email coordination.
expected_observation_points: S0 failure should localize HR tool argument/object-state mismatch.
S2 tool_state should separate HR tool findings from calendar/email coordination calls.
Closure claims should remain secondary unless explicit missing evidence is produced.
